response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional technical depth with 20 years of software engineering experience and relevant developer tools/infrastructure work, they fail to meet a critical minimum requirement: 2 years of documented people management experience. The resume shows no manager titles, no mention of direct reports, hiring, performance reviews, or team leadership—only individual contributor and staff-level positions. The role explicitly requires people-management experience as a core competency. Without this foundational leadership experience, the candidate is not qualified for a manager position, though they would be a strong fit for a senior staff engineer or technical lead role.
strengths: 20 years of software development experience with expertise in developer tools, CI/CD infrastructure, and testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Demonstrated technical depth across multiple platforms and languages, with staff-level positions showing capability for complex system design and architecture
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, talks, and teaching (Selenium course, Web Internals, FOSS4G presentation)
concerns: No documented people management experience despite role requiring minimum 2 years—no manager titles, direct reports, hiring decisions, or performance reviews mentioned in resume
Developer tools experience is spread across many domains rather than concentrated focus; CI/CD and testing infrastructure work spans only ~2 years vs. 3 required
No evidence of leading organizational change, strategic roadmap ownership, or cross-functional team alignment—critical responsibilities for manager role

key_factors.factor: Strong technical foundation and developer tools background
